meta.hardware: NVIDIA DGX Spark (GB10 Grace Blackwell, 128GB unified LPDDR5X ~273 GB/s, SM121)
meta.notes: Curated, Spark-specific tuning + model recommendations. The recommender diffs these against installed vLLM profiles and live state. Refresh via the research job (proposes updates for approval).

recommendations.title: Benchmark NVFP4 against FP8 — NVFP4 can be slower on GB10
recommendations.summary: On GB10 (SM121) a missing hardware instruction can make NVFP4 ~32% SLOWER than FP8 for the same model, despite fewer bits. Don't assume FP4 wins.
recommendations.action: For any profile you keep at NVFP4, stand up its FP8 twin and compare tok/s on your real dispatch pattern before committing.

sources.note: vLLM DGX Spark config + FP4/FP8 kernel notes

recommendations.title: Use the Marlin MoE backend — non-Marlin backends fail on GB10
recommendations.summary: Marlin is the only currently functional NVFP4/FP8-MoE backend on GB10; FlashInfer CUTLASS / TRTLLM get rejected at expert-count x intermediate-dim shape validation. Fires when a profile sets --moe-backend to something other than marlin (and doesn't force Marlin via env).
recommendations.action: Change --moe-backend to marlin (or set VLLM_TEST_FORCE_FP8_MARLIN=1 / VLLM_NVFP4_GEMM_BACKEND=marlin) in the flagged profile.

sources.note: Marlin mandatory for NVFP4 MoE on GB10

recommendations.title: Enable CUDA graphs (~60% decode speedup)
recommendations.summary: Nemotron Nano went 42 tok/s eager -> 66-67 tok/s with CUDA graphs on this box.
recommendations.action: Ensure CUDA graphs are not disabled (avoid --enforce-eager) in each profile.

recommendations.title: Enable prefix caching — free throughput
recommendations.summary: --enable-prefix-caching is free throughput for any workload with repeated system prompts (agents, routers, RAG).
recommendations.action: Add --enable-prefix-caching to profiles serving repeated system prompts.

recommendations.title: Raise gpu-memory-utilization — you are under-using 128GB
recommendations.summary: Default nano runs at 0.35 util. Community serves 16GB models with ~82GB free for KV cache; higher util = longer context + far higher batched throughput (Super NVFP4: 45 tok/s @8 streams -> 490 @128).
recommendations.action: For a solo-resident model, push --gpu-memory-utilization toward 0.80-0.90, leaving OS/services headroom.

recommendations.title: Consider Gemma 4 26B-A4B — the pragmatic Spark default
recommendations.summary: 16.5GB MoE footprint, multimodal (text/image/video), ~52 tok/s and 108+ tok/s with MTP speculative decoding, 82GB free for KV. Note: one head-to-head put Qwen 3.6 35B-A3B well ahead on quality (75% vs 46%), so pick per use-case.
recommendations.action: Add a Gemma 4 26B-A4B profile (NVFP4/FP8) for a fast multimodal default; benchmark quality vs your Qwen 3.6 A3B.

recommendations.title: Consider gpt-oss-120B (MXFP4) — largest-capable, MoE-light
recommendations.summary: 120B total but 128 experts / 4 active = ~5B active params/token, so it fits and runs ~65+ tok/s single-seq on GB10. Unlike the dense Super-120B you retired for overcommit, MoE active-param count keeps it tractable.
recommendations.action: Evaluate gpt-oss-120B MXFP4 for a high-capability profile; verify VRAM footprint fits your admission floor before enabling.

recommendations.title: Verify ARM64/CUDA vLLM image on suspect tags (GB10 is aarch64)
recommendations.summary: Default Docker Hub :latest is amd64; on GB10 that is an exec-format error at runtime. Only fires on suspect image tags (:latest or an explicit amd64 tag) — a running container already proves aarch64, so working profiles stay quiet. Community image nologik/vllm-dgx-spark targets TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST=12.1f / CUDA 13.
recommendations.action: Pin the start script to an aarch64/sm_121 image (an ARM vllm-openai build or nologik/vllm-dgx-spark).
